justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. BR-3 (daily limits) marked invariant: false and BR-7 (notification) marked invariant: false — both correct. BR-5 (sanctions) correctly marked invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limits explicitly surfaced as both an edge case and an escalation. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 72 falls within the 60-85 acceptable range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified and correctly classified as invariant or non-invariant
Notification rule (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification that it is business policy, not a state constraint
Sanctions screening (BR-5) correctly marked invariant: true with strong justification about criminal compliance
Timezone ambiguity explicitly called out in edge_cases and escalations with three concrete options and a recommendation
12 acceptance criteria — triple the minimum — covering KYC, limits, settlement, sanctions, lockout, notifications, and the consecutive-failure-reset edge case

BR-3 daily limits marked invariant: false with nuanced justification distinguishing the existence of limits (invariant) from the specific dollar amounts (configurable policy) — though marked the whole rule as false, which is a reasonable interpretation
Strong edge cases including T+1 business vs calendar day, sanctions re-screening between initiation and settlement, and locked account receiving funds
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated — acknowledges clear areas while flagging genuine specification gaps
Escalations are actionable with concrete options and recommendations, not just flagged as ambiguous
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified accounts throughout without conflating their limits

trials.justification: All 7 rules identified (BR-1 through BR-8) with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-6) included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as an edge case and escalation. 15 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level 72 falls within the 60-85 range with detailed assumptions listed. Bird correctly distinguishes regulatory invariants from operational/business policy rules. Additionally, BR-3 and BR-4 (daily limits) are marked invariant: false with the justification that specific dollar amounts are tunable parameters, which shows nuanced understanding of the distinction between the existence of limits (invariant) and the specific values (configurable).

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified and correctly classified — notification rule is invariant: false, sanctions and other regulatory rules are invariant: true
Timezone ambiguity explicitly called out both as an edge case and as a formal escalation with concrete options (UTC midnight, user-local, rolling window)
15 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, covering all major rules including edge boundaries like exactly $5,000 and exactly $1,000
Excellent edge case coverage beyond requirements — sanctions list updates mid-flight, self-transfers, partially-verified withdrawal ambiguity, consecutive login counter reset
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated with clearly articulated high and low confidence areas
Three formal escalations raised for genuine spec ambiguities, each with options and a recommendation — goes beyond minimum requirements

BR-3 and BR-4 split daily limits into two rules with invariant: false for both. The rubric expects daily limits marked as invariant: true for the existence of limits (with amounts being soft config). Bird's classification is defensible but treats the rules as the specific dollar amounts rather than the concept of tiered limits existing at all
